title: cereus jalapaensis
intro: Cereus jalapaensis, also known as the "Carrion Flower" due to its strong, putrid odor, is a species of columnar cactus native to Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Cereus jalapaensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antiseptic properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the plant's stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
